file.content:
# zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl -- idempotently provision
# BUJB_wsl_distribution by purging any prior state, installing an
# Ubuntu-24.04 seed, exporting it to a .tar, importing under the
# canonical name, then unregistering the seed and removing the .tar.
# Caller is bujb_caparison_windows phase 3, which has already asserted
# resolve + bubep_windows. Each step propagates failure via
# zbujb_admin_powershell + || buc_die.
zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  local -r z_distro_dir="${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
  local -r z_tar_path="${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}.tar"

  buc_step "  Stage WSL: ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} on ${BURN_HOST} (seed: ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution})"

  buc_step "  [1/6] Purge prior state (idempotent: unregister both, remove tar+dir)"
  local z_wsl_list=""
  z_wsl_list=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "wsl.exe --list --quiet") \
    || z_wsl_list=""
  if grep -qFx "${BUJB_wsl_distribution}" <<<"${z_wsl_list}"; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to unregister prior ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
  fi
  if grep -qFx "${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" <<<"${z_wsl_list}"; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to unregister prior ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"
  fi
  local z_tar_present z_dir_present
  z_tar_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_tar_path}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_tar_path}"
  z_dir_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_distro_dir}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_distro_dir}"
  if [[ "${z_tar_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove prior ${z_tar_path}"
  fi
  if [[ "${z_dir_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '${z_distro_dir}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove prior ${z_distro_dir}"
  fi

  buc_step "  [2/6] Ensure ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root} directory"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path '${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}' -Force | Out-Null" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}"

  buc_step "  [3/6] Install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed distribution"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed"

  buc_step "  [4/6] Export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} to ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [5/6] Import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} from ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} '${z_distro_dir}' '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [6/6] Cleanup: unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} and remove .tar"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"
  z_tar_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_tar_path}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_tar_path}"
  if [[ "${z_tar_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove ${z_tar_path}"
  fi

  buc_step "  Stage WSL succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Privileged SSH (thin admin-side pass-through)

# bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND... -- run an arbitrary command on the BURN
# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator
# prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires).
# Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand. Returns
# ssh's exit code.
bujb_privileged_ssh() {

# zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact -- exercise the locked-down workload
# SSH path end-to-end. A successful workload `ssh ... true` validates: the
# caparison-installed Match User block routing AuthorizedKeysFile to the
# absolute admin-owned path, the garrison-installed command= directive in
# that file, and the wsl.exe --user --exec --bash redirect into the
# workload's distribution. Only valid AFTER garrison-w has completed for
# this investiture; not called from caparison/garrison precondition paths.
zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  buc_step "  Fact: workload SSH round-trip via locked-down command= directive"
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"               \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                  \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"           \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"       \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}"         \
      true                                         \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}"               \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}"              \
    || buc_die "workload SSH round-trip: ssh as ${BUJB_workload_user} returned non-zero (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}) — caparison-windows Match block or garrison-w lockdown_rewrite may be malformed"
}

# bujb_invigilate_windows -- BUSJIW read-only host posture verification.
# Three fact groups: operator-handbook preconditions (op_facts), caparison
# deliverables (caparison_facts), and workload round-trip (roundtrip_fact).
# Round-trip fact is post-garrison-w only; callers that need to invoke
# invigilate before garrison-w (caparison preflight, garrison precondition)
# must call the helper functions directly rather than this full audit.
bujb_invigilate_windows() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_invigilate_windows: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  zbujb_invigilate_assert_platform bubep_windows

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  buc_step "  Fact: admin SSH reachable (key-only)"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "admin SSH session unreachable under key-only auth (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows succeeded"
}

  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \

content.text: Heading into BUSJGW edits for all six items. Starting with the largest restructure — step 11 four-session shape (#3).

input.old_string:
NOTE: All workload state changes occur within a *single*
SSH-as-workload session (step 11) terminated by `wsl.exe --shutdown`
as its final act, releasing the `NTUSER.DAT` hive before Windows
unloads the logon. This discipline prevents Windows User Profile
Service from demoting subsequent workload logons to numbered
fallback profile directories. See Rationale.
input.new_string:
NOTE: Workload provisioning runs as *four* atomic SSH-as-workload
sessions (step 11), each carrying a single cmdlet or native-binary
call per WSG WSp-105. The fourth session is `wsl.exe --shutdown`
alone, releasing the `NTUSER.DAT` hive before Windows unloads that
logon. Best-effort minimum-session-count: WSG-strict single-cmdlet
bodies and single-session-with-shutdown are mutually exclusive in
the transport stack, so multi-session demotion risk during sessions
1-3 is accepted; Layer 1 (caparison-installed absolute-path Match
block + admin-owned authorized_keys) is the auth defense if
demotion occurs. See Rationale.

content.text: Now ProfileList wording (#5):

input.old_string:
//axhos_step
. *Create Windows workload account*:
.. {busc_call}
`net.exe user /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes
${BUJB_workload_user}` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- provisions
{bujb_workload_user}
as a Windows local user and registers its SID under
`HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\ProfileList`
so Windows OpenSSH builds a complete logon session on inbound SSH
.. No Linux user is created inside admin's `rbtww-main` -- the
workload's Linux user is minted inside the workload's *own*
imported distribution in step 11
.. {busc_fatal}
on account-management failure
input.new_string:
//axhos_step
. *Create Windows workload account*:
.. {busc_call}
`net.exe user /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes
${BUJB_workload_user}` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- creates
{bujb_workload_user}
in the SAM database
(`HKLM\\SAM\\SAM\\Domains\\Account\\Users`). The
`HKLM\\...\\ProfileList\\<SID>` entry is *not* written by
`net.exe user /add`; User Profile Service populates it on the first
interactive logon, which the step 11 workload SSH session triggers
via `wsl.exe --import` and the surrounding logon ceremony
.. No Linux user is created inside admin's `rbtww-main` -- the
workload's Linux user is minted inside the workload's *own*
imported distribution in step 11
.. {busc_fatal}
on account-management failure

content.text: Now the big restructure — step 11 four-session shape (#3):

input.new_string:
//axhos_step
. *Provision workload's WSL (four atomic SSH-as-workload sessions per WSG WSp-105)*:
.. {busc_call}
*Session 1 (import)*: ssh as
{bujb_workload_user}
with
{burp_workload_key_file}
running `wsl.exe --import rbtww-main <workload-wsl-root>
«SEED_TARBALL»` -- registers the workload's own `rbtww-main` in
workload's `HKCU\\Lxss`; `<workload-wsl-root>` is a workload-writable
directory inside the workload's Windows profile. The bare entry at
the absolute-path authorized_keys authenticates this session
.. {busc_call}
*Session 2 (useradd)*: ssh as
{bujb_workload_user}
running `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root useradd
--create-home --shell /bin/bash ${BUJB_workload_user}` -- mints the
inner Linux workload user. `useradd` produces a locked-by-default
account per `/etc/shadow` convention (shadow entry `!` when `-p`
is not supplied); no separate `passwd --lock` session required
.. {busc_call}
*Session 3 (privkey)*: ssh as
{bujb_workload_user}
running `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root install -D
-m 600 -o ${BUJB_workload_user} -g ${BUJB_workload_user} /dev/stdin
/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519`, redirecting
{burp_workload_key_file}
to ssh's stdin -- the key flows through ssh FD 0 -> cmd.exe -> wsl.exe
-> Linux child, where `install -D /dev/stdin` sets mode and owner
atomically and creates the parent `.ssh/` directory. No plaintext
key on either side's disk, no remote temp file, no key material
in argv
.. {busc_call}
*Session 4 (shutdown)*: ssh as
{bujb_workload_user}
running `wsl.exe --shutdown` -- releases the WSL VM and helper-
process hive handles (wslhost.exe, wslservice) before this session's
Windows logon unloads. Best-effort defense against demotion
accumulation across the four sessions; Layer 1
({busn_caparison}-installed absolute-path Match block) is the auth
defense if a stray demotion occurs
.. {busc_call}
delete `«SEED_TARBALL»` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
.. {busc_fatal}
on any session's failure -- workload state partially provisioned
across namespaces; revalidate from a clean garrison

aiTitle: Reconcile Windows bash and spec drift items

input.old_string:
*Single-session-with-shutdown (step 11)*::
The session does `wsl --import` + inner provisioning + `wsl
--shutdown` in one logon. The shutdown is required as the
session's final act because `wsl --import` registers state in
canonical `HKCU\Lxss`, and a stray Windows User Profile Service
demotion during this session would relocate the registration to a
numbered fallback profile (`bujuw_user.<host>.NNN`). Auth would
still succeed (absolute-path authorized_keys bypasses `$HOME`),
but `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main` in the locked-down
`command=` would fail to find the distribution. The shutdown
releases the `NTUSER.DAT` hive cleanly before Windows unloads the
logon, closing the demotion path. The lockdown rewrite (step 12)
runs in admin context after the workload session exits -- no
in-session rewrite race.
input.new_string:
*Four-session shape with final shutdown (step 11)*::
WSG WSp-105 (one cmdlet or native binary per body) forbids the
compound session shape (wsl --import + useradd + install-D + wsl
--shutdown all on one workload SSH connection). Each cmdlet
therefore opens its own session. Across sessions 1-3, helper
processes (wslhost.exe, wslservice) spawned by `wsl --import`
outlive their parent session and lock the NTUSER.DAT hive of the
workload's logon, exposing the demotion path (User Profile Service
relocating the canonical profile to `bujuw_user.<host>.NNN` on the
next logon). Session 4 is `wsl.exe --shutdown` as its only act,
releasing the WSL VM and the helper-process hive handles before
that session's logon unloads -- best-effort cleanup that limits but
does not eliminate the demotion risk across sessions 1-3. Layer 1
({busn_caparison}-installed absolute-path Match block + admin-owned
authorized_keys) is the auth defense if demotion still occurs:
sshd reads as SYSTEM from the absolute path and never resolves the
workload's `$HOME`. The lockdown rewrite (step 12) runs in admin
context after session 4 exits -- no in-session rewrite race.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
//axhob_operation
{bust_caparison_windows}

NOTE: {busn_caparison} on Windows -- three phases. Phase 1: admin SSH
key trust + `sshd_config` hardening. Phase 2: key-only reconnect.
Phase 3: post-trust admin posture (WSL distribution, powercfg,
Tailscale service). Closes with {bust_invigilate_windows}.

NOTE: Nuclear -- every invocation purges and re-establishes the
caparison-managed posture; no skip-if-present logic.
{bust_handbook_windows} owns operator-manual posture; caparison owns
the idempotent admin-shell remainder.

NOTE: Phase 1's `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session
-- the post-restart disconnect is the expected phase boundary, not a
failure. PowerShell-side operations use `Get-Content`, `Set-Service`,
`Start-Service`, `Restart-Service`, `wsl.exe --import`; bash-side
BCG-compliant matching extracts effective `sshd_config` directives
from the raw bytes.

The {busn_caparison} operation on Windows:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node.

//axhoq_precondition
{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
Caparison presupposes this scope.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH session*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (steady-state: key via
{burp_privileged_key_file}; first run: TTY password)
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` for phase 1 operations
.. {busc_fatal}
on connection failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — derive admin pubkey*:
.. {busc_call}
`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file}
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
.. {busc_fatal}
on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — install admin pubkey*:
.. write `«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` to `administrators_authorized_keys` over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` as a bare key entry (no `command=`)
.. nuclear -- file replaced, not merged
.. {busc_fatal}
on write failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
.. {busc_call}
`icacls` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- ACL restricted to SYSTEM and
Administrators (Windows OpenSSH refuses the file otherwise);
idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `icacls` failure -- phase 2 reconnect would fail

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — write hardened sshd_config directives*:
.. write `PubkeyAuthentication yes`, `PasswordAuthentication no`,
`PermitEmptyPasswords no` to `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`;
idempotent
.. ensure `sshd_config` carries a `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}`
block routing `AuthorizedKeysFile` to
`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys` --
absolute path decouples sshd's workload authorized_keys lookup from
workload's `$HOME` (so a future profile demotion cannot lock out
auth); parallel to the built-in `Match Group administrators` block
routing to `administrators_authorized_keys`. Idempotent
.. provision the parent directory
`__PROGRAMDATA__\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}\\` over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` with ACL: admins/SYSTEM Full Control,
inheritance disabled, no workload grant -- sshd reads as
`NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM` and accesses the trust file under its own
identity, parallel to `administrators_authorized_keys` ACL
discipline. Idempotent. The directory persists across
{busn_garrison} destroy-and-recreate cycles -- garrison rewrites
the file contents but never touches the directory
.. {busc_fatal}
on write or ACL failure -- restart would still serve unhardened auth
or workload auth path would not resolve

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — verify hardened directives took*:
.. {busc_call}
PowerShell `Get-Content` on `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
directive values (BCG-compliant matching), AND contains a
`Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block whose `AuthorizedKeysFile`
resolves to
`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
.. {busc_fatal}
on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`sshd -t` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- penultimate atomic op of phase 1
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*:
.. {busc_call}
`Restart-Service sshd` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- final atomic op
of phase 1
.. {busc_fatal}
on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
expected phase boundary)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — reconnect under key-only auth*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using {burp_privileged_key_file}
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` for phase 3 operations
.. {busc_fatal}
on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
session); recover via operator's local console

//axhos_step
. *Preflight — verify operator-precondition facts before phase 3*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} operator-precondition fact subset
(`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` registry, `PlatformAoAcOverride`
registry, Tailscale service registered) over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`
.. {busc_store}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope; preflight fails fast
before the multi-minute WSL stage so missing operator posture does
not waste a download cycle

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
.. {busc_call}
`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` to register `rbtww-main` under the admin user
(canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction})
.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
.. {busc_fatal}
on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
.. {busc_call}
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
`powercfg /hibernate off` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
.. {busc_call}
`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)

//axhos_step
. *Post-completion — verify caparison deliverables*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} caparison-deliverable fact subset
(Tailscale service `Automatic`, standby-timeout policy = 0 AC+DC,
hibernate disabled, `rbtww-main` WSL distribution registered) over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`. Operator-precondition facts already verified
at preflight do not re-run, and admin SSH is not re-probed because
phase 2 just established it
.. {busc_store}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim

//axhog_guarantee
On success:

* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`, AND a
  `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block routing
  `AuthorizedKeysFile` to the admin-owned absolute path
  `__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`.
* `__PROGRAMDATA__\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}\\` directory
  exists with admins-FullControl + workload-Read ACL, ready for
  {busn_garrison} to write the trust file content (the file itself
  is garrison's responsibility, not caparison's).
* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user.
* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
* {bust_invigilate_windows}'s caparison-deliverable fact subset
  confirms each post-trust posture fact (operator-precondition facts
  were verified at the phase 2/3 preflight gate); a standalone
  {bust_invigilate_windows} run after caparison succeeds asserts the
  full fact list end-to-end.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the post-completion deliverables-subset
{bust_invigilate_windows} passes. Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure
(admin SSH, pubkey, ACL, `sshd_config`, `sshd -t`), phase-2 reconnect
failure, preflight failure (operator-precondition facts), any phase-3
op failure (`wsl --import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
deliverables-subset failure.

input.old_string:
# Windows-side install directory housing both seed and canonical
# distribution VHD trees plus the intermediate .tar export.
BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root='C:\WSL'

# WSL artifacts.
# Seed tarball lives under C:\WSL (caparison-windows-created;
# inherits BUILTIN\Users:ReadAndExecute + Authenticated Users:Modify
# ACL so the SSH-as-workload `wsl --import` reads it without an icacls
# fix-up step). Writing the seed under the workload profile would
# inherit admin's logon-SID ACL excluding the workload user, since
# admin runs the wsl --export. The workload's installed-VHD root
# stays under the workload profile (workload owns it; created by the
# workload's own wsl --import).
input.new_string:

# the path is unambiguously project-controlled rather than reading
# as a Microsoft-default location (the prior C:\WSL value did).
BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root='C:\bujb-wsl'

# WSL artifacts.
# Seed tarball lives under C:\bujb-wsl (caparison-windows-created;
# inherits BUILTIN\Users:ReadAndExecute from C:\ default ACL, which

# seed without an icacls fix-up step). Writing the seed under the
# workload profile would inherit admin's logon-SID ACL excluding
# the workload user, since admin runs the wsl --export. The
# workload's installed-VHD root stays under the workload profile
# (workload owns it; created by the workload's own wsl --import).

content.text: Now drop the round-trip helper and its caller:

input.old_string:
# zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact -- exercise the locked-down workload
# SSH path end-to-end. A successful workload `ssh ... true` validates: the
# caparison-installed Match User block routing AuthorizedKeysFile to the
# absolute admin-owned path, the garrison-installed command= directive in
# that file, and the wsl.exe --user --exec --bash redirect into the
# workload's distribution. Only valid AFTER garrison-w has completed for
# this investiture; not called from caparison/garrison precondition paths.
zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  buc_step "  Fact: workload SSH round-trip via locked-down command= directive"
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"               \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                  \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"           \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"       \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}"         \
      true                                         \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}"               \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}"              \
    || buc_die "workload SSH round-trip: ssh as ${BUJB_workload_user} returned non-zero (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}) — caparison-windows Match block or garrison-w lockdown_rewrite may be malformed"
}

# bujb_invigilate_windows -- BUSJIW read-only host posture verification.
# Three fact groups: operator-handbook preconditions (op_facts), caparison
# deliverables (caparison_facts), and workload round-trip (roundtrip_fact).
# Round-trip fact is post-garrison-w only; callers that need to invoke
# invigilate before garrison-w (caparison preflight, garrison precondition)
# must call the helper functions directly rather than this full audit.
bujb_invigilate_windows() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_invigilate_windows: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  zbujb_invigilate_assert_platform bubep_windows

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  buc_step "  Fact: admin SSH reachable (key-only)"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "admin SSH session unreachable under key-only auth (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows succeeded"
}
input.new_string:
# bujb_invigilate_windows -- BUSJIW read-only host posture verification.
# Two fact groups: operator-handbook preconditions (op_facts) and caparison
# deliverables (caparison_facts). Round-trip validation under the locked-
# down command= directive is NOT an invigilate fact; the operator's first
# workload-SSH invocation post-garrison (buw-jwk / buw-jwc / buw-jws) IS
# the round-trip. An explicit invigilate fact for this assertion was
# retired as overdesign once those operational tabtargets existed.
bujb_invigilate_windows() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_invigilate_windows: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  zbujb_invigilate_assert_platform bubep_windows

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  buc_step "  Fact: admin SSH reachable (key-only)"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "admin SSH session unreachable under key-only auth (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows succeeded"
}

content.text: Now Phase 3 obliterate — collapse to single Remove-LocalUser -EAS:

input.old_string:
##############
  # Phase 3 — SAM scrub (Windows local user)

  buc_step "    [Phase 3] Probe Get-LocalUser ${BUJB_workload_user}"
  local z_sam=""
  z_sam=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \
      "Get-LocalUser -Name '${BUJB_workload_user}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name") \
    || z_sam=""
  if test -n "${z_sam}"; then
    buc_step "    [Phase 3] Remove-LocalUser ${BUJB_workload_user}"
    zbujb_obliterate_run "p3-remove-localuser" \
        zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-LocalUser -Name '${BUJB_workload_user}'" \
      || buc_die "obliterate phase 3: Remove-LocalUser failed for ${BUJB_workload_user}"
  fi
input.new_string:
##############
  # Phase 3 — SAM scrub (Windows local user)
  #
  # Collapsed dispatch per WSG WSp-108 nuclear-cleanup carve-out: narrow
  # failure spectrum at this callsite (admin context, SAM database, fixed
  # workload user name); subsequent step's New-LocalUser provides the
  # downstream verification by erroring on "account already exists" if a
  # silent partial cleanup left a SID behind.

  buc_step "    [Phase 3] Remove-LocalUser ${BUJB_workload_user} (collapsed, -EAS)"

Phase 3 stage step prose updated to anchor 'C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main' as the wsl --import destination. BUSJCW Guarantee section gains a new bullet for the C:\\bujb-wsl directory and rewrites the rbtww-main bullet to name the absolute install path. BUSJGW step 3 (Export seed) rewritten to name C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-seed.tar explicitly, with inline rationale documenting why the seed cannot live under the workload's Windows profile (admin's logon-SID inheritance excludes workload) and why C:\\bujb-wsl works (caparison-provisioned + C:\\-inherited Users:ReadAndExecute). The seed-tarball variable « SEED_TARBALL » now stores the literal C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-seed.tar value rather than the abstract 'chosen path'. Item 2 (workload-Read ACL grant on workload authkeys directory): operator's prior Q3=clean decision (drop the workload-Read grant; sshd reads as NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM and accesses workload's authorized_keys under its own identity, parallel to administrators_authorized_keys's admin+SYSTEM-only ACL) was already implemented in bash at zbujb_caparison_windows phase-1 icacls call but the BUSJCW and BUSJGW specs still carried the workload-Read mention. BUSJCW Phase 1 sshd-config step's ACL substep rewritten: 'admins/SYSTEM Full Control, inheritance disabled, no workload grant' with the parallel-to-administrators_authorized_keys rationale stated inline. BUSJCW Guarantee bullet for the authkeys directory rewritten in the same shape. BUSJGW precondition that references the caparison-provisioned authkeys directory rewritten to drop the workload-Read claim and add the same SYSTEM-identity rationale. BUSJGW step 10 (Place bare workload trust) rewritten to drop the workload-Read mention from its closing clause about the caparison-provisioned directory. Item 3 (single-session vs four-session): operator's prior Q1=(a) decision (WSG-strict + minimum-session-count + final wsl --shutdown + reliance on Layer 1 absolute-path Match block) was already implemented in bash as four atomic single-cmdlet workload-SSH session functions (zbujb_garrison_w_session_{import,useradd,privkey,shutdown}) but the BUSJGW spec still described step 11 as a single SSH-as-workload session with a 'this is the *only* SSH-as-workload session inside garrison-w' assertion. BUSJGW step 11 (Provision workload's WSL) restructured: title changes to 'Provision workload's WSL (four atomic SSH-as-workload sessions per WSG WSp-105)' and the body is rewritten as four distinctly-named substeps: Session 1 (import) — wsl.exe --import; Session 2 (useradd) — wsl --user root useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash, with inline note that useradd produces a locked-by-default account per /etc/shadow convention so no separate passwd --lock session is required (collapsed five sessions to four); Session 3 (privkey) — wsl --user root install -D -m 600 -o <user> -g <user> /dev/stdin /home/<user>/.ssh/id_ed25519 with BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE redirected to ssh stdin (no plaintext on disk, no remote temp file, no key material in argv); Session 4 (shutdown) — wsl.exe --shutdown alone, releasing the WSL VM and helper-process hive handles before this session's logon unloads. The «WORKLOAD_SESSION» variable was dropped entirely from this step since each session is a single cmdlet with no shared state across substeps within a session. The closing admin-context delete of «SEED_TARBALL» retained on the trailing substep. busc_fatal scope updated to 'on any session's failure'. Head NOTE about workload provisioning rewritten: replaced 'single SSH-as-workload session (step 11) terminated by wsl.exe --shutdown as its final act' with 'four atomic SSH-as-workload sessions (step 11), each carrying a single cmdlet or native-binary call per WSG WSp-105' and naming Layer 1 (absolute-path Match block) as the auth defense if demotion occurs across the four sessions. Rationale section's 'Single-session-with-shutdown' entry renamed to 'Four-session shape with final shutdown' and rewritten to explicate the WSp-105-vs-single-session mutual exclusion in the transport stack, name the helper-process hive-leak mechanism that exposes the demotion path across sessions 1-3, document the best-effort scope of session 4's shutdown, and re-anchor Layer 1's role as the auth defense if demotion still occurs. Item 4 (BUSJIW round-trip fact): operator chose to drop the dead-code zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact helper rather than wire it as a garrison-w post-completion step. The function (lines 2090-2110) and its caller from bujb_invigilate_windows (line 2136) deleted; the surrounding comment on bujb_invigilate_windows rewritten from three-fact-groups to two-fact-groups (op_facts + caparison_facts) with explicit rationale that the operator's first workload-SSH invocation post-garrison (buw-jwk / buw-jwc / buw-jws) IS the round-trip validation. BUSJGW head NOTE about round-trip validation rewritten: replaced 'Round-trip validation under the locked-down directive is deferred to invigilate-windows as a post-completion fact; garrison-w does not self-validate' with 'Garrison-w does not self-validate the locked-down round-trip. The operator's first workload-SSH invocation post-garrison IS the round-trip validation; an explicit invigilate fact for this assertion was retired as overdesign.' BUSJIW unchanged (the spec never had a round-trip step to remove). Closes the cb94ae2c paddock claim 'round-trip validation deferred to invigilate-windows' as retired-overdesign rather than load-bearing-fact. Item 5 (BUSJGW step 9 ProfileList mention): the spec inherited a Microsoft-mechanism error — net.exe user /add creates the SAM entry (HKLM\\SAM\\SAM\\Domains\\Account\\Users) but does NOT register the SID under HKLM\\...\\ProfileList. ProfileList registration is the User Profile Service's responsibility on the first interactive logon; the manual ProfileList write attempted by an earlier spec draft was vestigial (registered the SID but did not call Userenv.dll CreateProfile to create the profile-directory skeleton) and was dropped in commit a69f25fb. BUSJGW step 9 (Create Windows workload account) rewritten: the net.exe substep's trailing rationale now reads 'creates {bujb_workload_user} in the SAM database (HKLM\\SAM\\SAM\\Domains\\Account\\Users). The HKLM\\...\\ProfileList\\<SID> entry is NOT written by net.exe user /add; User Profile Service populates it on the first interactive logon, which the step 11 workload SSH session triggers via wsl.exe --import and the surrounding logon ceremony.' Bash unchanged (the bash never tried to write ProfileList — the manual write was dropped in a69f25fb). Item 6 (Phase 2 LIKE pattern prefix-collision): operator's prior fix (bash WMI Filter uses exact-match canonical OR LIKE-dot for demoted, tightened from the spec's unbounded '<user>%' which prefix-matches unrelated accounts like bujuw_user_alt) was already implemented in bash at the Win32_UserProfile probe call but the BUSJGW Phase 2 spec still had the unbounded '<user>%' form. BUSJGW Phase 2 axhos_step's probe substep rewritten to match bash's exact-match-OR-LIKE-dot shape, with inline note that 'the unbounded <user>% form is forbidden because it would prefix-match unrelated accounts like ${BUJB_workload_user}_alt.' Bash unchanged. Cross-cutting bash polish: the obliterate function's leading comment block (Phases 1-5 summary) updated to reflect the new Phase 3 collapsed shape (Remove-LocalUser -EAS per WSG WSp-108 carve-out, narrow failure spectrum at SAM callsite, downstream New-LocalUser catches silent partial cleanup), the new Phase 5 collapsed shape (userdel exit-code-6 tolerance per the orchestration-style memo's POSIX false-branches principle, NOT WSp-108 since userdel is a Linux native binary), and the retained Phase 4 Cygwin probe-then-act shape (arbitrary filesystem path; WSp-108 main rule, no carve-out — narrow-failure-spectrum condition fails for arbitrary filesystem paths with NTFS ACL friction / junction loops / file-locked-by-process). bujb_jurisdiction.sh obliterate Phase 3 (lines around 833-846) rewritten: Get-LocalUser probe + conditional Remove-LocalUser collapsed to a single Remove-LocalUser -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue dispatch with the WSG WSp-108 carve-out reference inline in the comment block, and the busc_fatal trailer scope narrowed to 'SSH transport failed' (cmdlet-level absent-state is suppressed by design, not fatal). bujb_jurisdiction.sh obliterate Phase 5 (lines around 901-925) rewritten: wsl-id probe + conditional userdel collapsed to a single wsl --user root userdel dispatch with bash-side exit-code capture and a 0-or-6 tolerance check (any other non-zero is fatal). rm -rf retained as unconditional follow-up (rm -rf is idempotent on absent paths). Phase 4 Cygwin home probe-then-act left intact per the prior WSp-108 main-rule decision. Pace-affiliated commit per the pace docket's interleaved-spec-and-bash sanction. Gates: bash -n on bujb_jurisdiction.sh OK; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh passes 5 fixtures / 28 cases; tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.handbook-render.sh passes 15/15; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh passes 4 fixtures (enrollment-validation, regime-validation, regime-smoke, handbook-render). Field exercise on bujn-winpc remains deferred to the operator's next garrison-w invocation in a clean chat. All six drift items from commit a69f25fb's surfaced backlog resolved; spec and bash now coherent for the Windows-only scope of pace ₢A-ABG (windows-setup-first-time-debug)."}

content.text:
Two notches landed (dc7c101c WSp-108 carve-out, e9e037e1 six drift items reconciled). Pace `₢A-ABG` is now at a clean mid-cycle handoff point — bash and spec coherent for Windows-only scope; field exercise on bujn-winpc is the next move.

Here's the clean-chat prompt:

---

```
Mount via JJK protocol to surface the pace docket — its "Done when" clause is
"Operator declares Windows setup converged with spec and code coherent against
observed Windows OpenSSH behavior on bujn-winpc." The pace's Character clause
sanctions interleaved spec and bash edits; BUS*.adoc is working-draft, not
load-bearing yet.

This pace is mid-cycle. Two pace-affiliated commits just landed in a prior chat
that you should read in full:

1. dc7c101c — WSp-108 nuclear-cleanup absent-state collapse carve-out minted in
   WSG. Three load-bearing conditions for permitted dispatch-side -ErrorAction
   SilentlyContinue: narrow failure spectrum at the callsite, downstream
   verification, single suppression point in named form. Footnoted the
   orchestration-style memo's PowerShell collapse rows to defer to WSp-108.
   BUSJGW Phase 3 collapse anchored to the carve-out; Phase 4 Cygwin reverted
   to probe-then-act (carve-out doesn't qualify for arbitrary filesystem
   paths — operator's call: when in doubt, make it fatal and address on debug).

2. e9e037e1 — reconciled six drift items from a69f25fb's spec-drift trailer.
   Renamed C:\WSL to C:\bujb-wsl so the directory reads as project-controlled
   rather than Microsoft-default; BUSJCW gained a discrete provisioning step
   for it. Dropped the workload-Read grant from the workload-authkeys
   directory ACL (sshd reads as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM, parallel to
   administrators_authorized_keys). Restructured BUSJGW step 11 from "single
   SSH-as-workload session" to "four atomic SSH-as-workload sessions per
   WSp-105" reflecting operator's Q1=(a) — WSG-strict and
   single-session-with-shutdown are mutually exclusive in the transport stack;
   session 4 is wsl.exe --shutdown alone; Layer 1 absolute-path Match block
   is the auth defense if multi-session demotion occurs. Dropped the
   dead-code zbujb_invigilate_windows_roundtrip_fact and its caller —
   operator's first workload-SSH invocation post-garrison (buw-jwk / buw-jwc
   / buw-jws) IS the round-trip; the cb94ae2c paddock claim retired as
   overdesign. Corrected BUSJGW step 9 ProfileList wording (net.exe creates
   the SAM entry; ProfileList registration is User Profile Service's
   responsibility on first interactive logon). Tightened the BUSJGW Phase 2
   WMI Filter from unbounded LIKE '<user>%' (prefix-matched unrelated
   accounts) to exact-match canonical OR LIKE '<user>.%' for demoted dirs.

State of play at mount:

- BUSJGW, BUSJCW, BUSJIW, and bujb_jurisdiction.sh coherent for Windows-only
  scope of this pace.
- Gates green: bash -n, tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh (5 fixtures / 28 cases),
  tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.handbook-render.sh (15/15),
  tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh (4 fixtures).
- Field exercise on bujn-winpc not yet attempted in this round of changes.
  Pre-existing C:\WSL on bujn-winpc from prior garrison-w attempts will be
  orphaned by the rename to C:\bujb-wsl; caparison-windows is nuclear so the
  next caparison pass creates the new dir, but old C:\WSL may want manual
  cleanup if disk hygiene matters.

Read in order:

1. The pace docket and the heat paddock (jjx_orient surfaces both via the
   officium gazette).
2. Commit message of e9e037e1 in full — six-item reconciliation with the
   per-item rationale chain.
3. Commit message of dc7c101c in full — WSp-108 carve-out resolution
   under interleaved WSG/memo/spec edits.
4. Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc — current spec exemplar
   under the orchestration-style discipline + Q1=(a) four-session shape.
5. Tools/buk/vov_veiled/WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md — especially WSp-105
   (one cmdlet per SSH body), WSp-108 (error-suppression rule with the
   new nuclear-cleanup carve-out exception), WSt-105 (exit-code
   propagation across cmd.exe/wsl.exe/cygwin/PS layers), and the
   Capture-Decide-Dispatch section.
6. Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJCW-CaparisonWindows.adoc — sibling spec;
   recently gained the discrete C:\bujb-wsl provisioning step.
7. Memos/memo-20260511-orchestration-style-axla-draft.md — orchestration-
   style discipline draft with the WSp-108 PowerShell-row footnote.

Next move is the field exercise on bujn-winpc. The four-session restructure
has not been exercised live; the multi-session demotion-risk discipline is
known-imperfect by design (Layer 1 Match block defends auth-survival if
demotion accumulates across sessions 1-3, but the empirical demotion
behavior under the new four-session shape on bujn-winpc is what this pace's
"Done when" clause is waiting on). New bugs are expected; the docket
sanctions interleaved spec+bash edits as they surface.

Scope: Windows-only — garrison-w, caparison-windows, invigilate-windows in
bujb_jurisdiction.sh, plus BUSJGW, BUSJCW, BUSJIW. Out of scope per the
heat plan: garrison-c (Cygwin), garrison-b (Linux/Mac), handbook recast,
comment annotations — each gets its own pace.

Out-of-scope adjacent: a separate heat ₣BG (rbk-mvp-3-orchestration-scrub)
covers sibling orchestration spec normalization. Don't touch sibling adoc
files (BUSJCM/CL, BUSJIM/IL, BUSJGC, BUSJGB) as part of this pace.

When ready: open officium, mount the pace, read materials in the order
above, then engage the operator's bujn-winpc field exercise plan.
```

---

Ready when you are. Two notes for your eyes only (not for the next chat):

1. The pace docket and paddock haven't been touched in this chat — they read accurately for where we are now, so no `jjx_paddock` setter needed before the handoff.
2. If you exercise garrison-w on bujn-winpc and uncover new shape problems, the chat continuing from the prompt above can iterate freely within the pace's interleaved-edits sanction. If something surfaces that's clearly out of pace scope (e.g., a sibling-spec issue or a handbook recast trigger), surface it as a spook rather than absorbing into this pace.
